ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.CfnAbout: An AWS Verified Access endpoint specifies the application that AWS Verified Access provides access to. It must be attached to an AWS Verified Access group. An AWS Verified Access endpoint must also have an attached access policy before you attached it to a group.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.ApplicationDo.Description: The DNS name for users to reach your application.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.AttachmentTyp.Description: The type of attachment used to provide connectivity between the AWS Verified Access endpoint and the application.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.Description.Description: A description for the AWS Verified Access endpoint.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.DomainCertifi.Description: The ARN of a public TLS/SSL certificate imported into or created with ACM.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.EndpointDomai.Description: A custom identifier that is prepended to the DNS name that is generated for the endpoint.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.EndpointType.Description: The type of AWS Verified Access endpoint. Incoming application requests will be sent to an IP address, load balancer or a network interface depending on the endpoint type specified.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.LoadBalancerO.Description: The load balancer details if creating the AWS Verified Access endpoint as `load-balancer`type.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.NetworkInterf.Description: The options for network-interface type endpoint.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.PolicyEnabled.Description: The status of the Verified Access policy.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.SecurityGroup.Description: The IDs of the security groups for the endpoint.

ec2.verifiedaccessendpoint.VerifiedAcces.Description: The ID of the AWS Verified Access group.
